summary: A solid soundboard recording with some mix quirks and cuts, highlighted by a stellar Scarlet Begonias→Fire on the Mountain but overall an average two‑hour show.
review: The dsbd.miller source captures a lengthy Boston run with decent clarity, though the reviewer notes a few missing sections, muffed verses and low levels on acoustic parts. Musically the night shines during the early second set – especially the Scarlet Begonias→Fire on the Mountain transition and a beautiful Slipknot! – but many other numbers are described as average or sloppy. Overall sentiment is mixed, reflecting both strong moments and noticeable flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is rated around average‑good with standout songs but several mediocre sections; recording is decent soundboard but has cuts and mix issues, yielding a combined rating in the low‑mid 3s.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Polished dsbd mix but suffers from missing intro of Scarlet Begonias→Fire, lowered acoustic levels on Me & My Uncle, muffed verses on So Many Roads, and occasional matrix‑style ambience. Overall clear enough for listening, though not flawless.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is uptempo and uneven, opening with a Help → Slipknot! → Franklin’s sequence; the second set stretches to two hours, delivering an early high point with Scarlet Begonias→Fire before slipping into mixed material and a long, meandering Space.

band_member_comments.Phil: Described as "Lovecraftian" on Tom Thumb's, suggesting an imaginative bass approach.
band_member_comments.Bob: Plays acoustic on Me & My Uncle, though his part is turned down in the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboardist) praised for great work on Slipknot! and overall contributions.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mixes drums differently on the OFF version; SBD provides a more accurate representation of the live drum sound.
